---
title: "Clovis Auction · This Week"
subtitle: "Producer reference card · Livestock Marketing Alternatives Explorer"
format:
  typst:
    papersize: us-letter
    margin:
      top: 0.5in
      bottom: 0.4in
      left: 0.65in
      right: 0.65in
    fontsize: 9.5pt
    section-numbering: ""
toc: false
execute:
  freeze: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: load-and-shape

# Reuse the cash-chart pages' loader — combined Era B + MARS Clovis
# snapshot, M&L 1 grade only, 100-lb bins. By using the same loader
# and the same filters we guarantee the producer card and the live
# charts can never disagree about what "this week's M&L 1 prices" are.

from pathlib import Path
import sys
import pandas as pd

PROCESSED = Path("..") / "data" / "processed"
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path("..").resolve()))
from pipelines.clovis.load import load_clovis_combined  # noqa: E402

_loaded = load_clovis_combined(PROCESSED)
if _loaded is None:
    raise RuntimeError(
        "No Clovis snapshot found at ../data/processed/. "
        "Run the Clovis pipeline before rendering the one-pager."
    )
df = _loaded.df

slim = df[
    (df["class"].isin(["Steers", "Heifers"]))
    & (df["frame"] == "Medium and Large")
    & (df["muscle_grade"] == "1")
].copy()
slim["auction_date"] = pd.to_datetime(slim["auction_date"])
# Derive 100-lb bin from weight_break_low (preferred) or avg_weight (fallback
# for the older MARS rows pre-2024 that lacked weight_break_low/high).
wb = slim["weight_break_low"]
aw = slim["avg_weight"]
raw_weight = wb.fillna(aw)
slim = slim[raw_weight.notna()].copy()
slim["bin_lo"] = ((raw_weight[raw_weight.notna()] // 100) * 100).astype(int)
slim = slim[(slim["bin_lo"] >= 300) & (slim["bin_lo"] <= 800)]
# en-dash to match the chart pages' visual convention
slim["weight_bin"] = slim["bin_lo"].apply(lambda lo: f"{lo}–{lo+99} lb")

BIN_ORDER = [f"{lo}–{lo+99} lb" for lo in range(300, 900, 100)]

# "This week" = latest auction_date in the snapshot. Anchoring on the data
# rather than today() avoids an empty card on weeks where the BLS pipeline
# is fresh but the Clovis pipeline hasn't published yet.
latest_date = slim["auction_date"].max()
this_week = slim[slim["auction_date"] == latest_date].copy()
n_lots_this_week = int(this_week["head_count"].fillna(0).sum())
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# Header — vintage stamp first so a reader skimming the printed card knows
# at a glance how fresh the data is.
from datetime import datetime, timezone

print(f"### Auction date: **{latest_date.strftime('%A, %B %-d, %Y')}**\n")
print(
    f"This card summarizes the most recent Clovis Livestock Auction "
    f"feeder-cattle report (USDA-AMS slug `AMS_1781`). All prices are "
    f"Medium-and-Large frame, muscle grade 1, $/cwt, **nominal**. "
    f"This week's reported lots in the M&L 1 sample: "
    f"**{n_lots_this_week:,} head**. The full interactive site at "
    f"[lma-explorer.github.io](https://lma-explorer.github.io) adds "
    f"weight-bin × class × week-of-year history, CPI-deflated views, "
    f"and basis to the CME GF feeder-cattle settle.\n"
)
```

#### This week's prices ($/cwt, nominal)

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

# Per (weight_bin, class), report the head-weighted mean price_avg across
# all M&L 1 lots that cleared this auction. Multiple lots in the same bin
# get weighted by head_count so a 200-head pen counts more than a 30-head
# pen — this is the same convention the live charts' aggregation uses.
table_lines = [
    "| Weight bin | Steers (avg) | Heifers (avg) |",
    "|---|---:|---:|",
]
for wb_label in BIN_ORDER:
    row_cells = []
    for cls in ("Steers", "Heifers"):
        cell = this_week[
            (this_week["class"] == cls)
            & (this_week["weight_bin"] == wb_label)
        ]
        if cell.empty:
            row_cells.append("—")
            continue
        # Head-weighted mean. Falls back to simple mean if head_count is
        # missing for every lot in this bin (rare; would only happen for
        # very old Era B rows without head_count parsed).
        hc = cell["head_count"].fillna(0)
        if hc.sum() > 0:
            avg = (cell["price_avg"] * hc).sum() / hc.sum()
        else:
            avg = cell["price_avg"].mean()
        row_cells.append(f"\\${avg:.2f}")
    table_lines.append(f"| {wb_label} | {row_cells[0]} | {row_cells[1]} |")
print("\n".join(table_lines))
```

#### Last 26 auction weeks

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: mini-charts
#| fig-width: 7.0
#| fig-height: 2.2

# Two side-by-side panels (Steers, Heifers), each with one line per
# 100-lb weight bin (six bins). Same color palette as the seasonality and
# weekly-trends pages so a reader who toggles between the live site and
# the printed card sees consistent visual identity per weight class.
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates

recent = slim[
    slim["auction_date"] >= (latest_date - pd.Timedelta(weeks=26))
].copy()
agg = (
    recent.groupby(["auction_date", "class", "weight_bin"], as_index=False)
    ["price_avg"].mean()
)

COLOR_BY_BIN = {
    "300–399 lb": "#6a3d9a",
    "400–499 lb": "#000000",
    "500–599 lb": "#2ca02c",
    "600–699 lb": "#d17a00",
    "700–799 lb": "#1f77b4",
    "800–899 lb": "#8b0000",
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7.0, 2.2), sharey=True)
for ax_idx, cls in enumerate(("Steers", "Heifers")):
    ax = axes[ax_idx]
    sub = agg[agg["class"] == cls]
    for wb_label in BIN_ORDER:
        b = sub[sub["weight_bin"] == wb_label].sort_values("auction_date")
        if not b.empty:
            ax.plot(
                b["auction_date"], b["price_avg"],
                marker=".", linewidth=1.0, markersize=3,
                label=wb_label, color=COLOR_BY_BIN[wb_label],
            )
    ax.set_title(f"NM Clovis {cls}", fontsize=10)
    if ax_idx == 0:
        ax.set_ylabel("$/cwt", fontsize=9)
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%b"))
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=8)

# Single shared legend below the two panels keeps each panel as wide as
# possible.
handles, labels = axes[1].get_legend_handles_labels()
fig.legend(
    handles, labels, ncol=6, fontsize=7,
    loc="lower center", bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.05),
    frameon=False,
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

#### How to read this card

- **Prices shown** are head-weighted averages for the latest reported auction date, M&L 1 grade only. "—" means no M&L 1 lots cleared in that weight bin this week.
- **The 26-week mini-charts** plot one point per auction × bin × class for the past six months. Useful for *"is this week high, low, or middle vs. recent history?"*
- **Out-of-bin lots** (frames other than M&L, muscle grades 2 or 1-2, weights below 300 or above 899 lb) are preserved in the underlying parquet but suppressed here for clarity.
- **For seasonality and basis** (CME GF cash-vs-futures gap), see the live site — those views require the full interactive cross-year aggregation that doesn't fit a single sheet.

#### Methodology and citation

- **Live site**: [lma-explorer.github.io](https://lma-explorer.github.io)
- **Methodology**: [lma-explorer.github.io/methodology](https://lma-explorer.github.io/methodology)
- **Data source**: USDA-AMS Market News, slug `AMS_1781` via the [MARS API](https://mymarketnews.ams.usda.gov/mars-api). Public domain ([17 U.S.C. § 105](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/105)).
- **License**: Content CC-BY-4.0; code MIT.
- **Citation**: see [CITATION.cff](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/CITATION.cff).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# Footer with the snapshot vintage + render UTC. Lets a printed card
# stand on its own as a citable artifact.
print(
    f"\n*Card generated {datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')}; "
    f"data vintage anchored on auction date {latest_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}.*\n"
)
```
